description_long: This object is a classic pipe wrench, featuring a robust metal head with an adjustable jaw mechanism and a long, cylindrical wooden handle. The head is composed of dark, heavy metal with visible gear teeth and a knurled adjustment knob, allowing the jaw to open and close for gripping pipes or fittings. The handle is made of polished wood, smoothly finished and slightly tapered for a comfortable grip, with a metallic collar connecting it to the head. The wrench's jaws are thick and curved, designed to clamp securely onto round surfaces, and the adjustment mechanism is prominently visible on the side. The metal surfaces show a matte, slightly worn finish, indicating frequent use, while the wooden handle retains a rich, brown hue. This tool is commonly found in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or construction sites, where it is used for plumbing and mechanical tasks.
description: A heavy-duty pipe wrench with a dark metal adjustable head and a smooth, brown wooden handle. The tool shows signs of use and is designed for gripping and turning pipes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded end of the metal head and the alignment of the wooden handle behind it.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the wrench, the adjustable jaw, and the detailed gear mechanism.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden handle and the back of the adjustment mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the wrench, including the jaw and adjustment screw.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle, smoothly finished, slightly tapered, with a metallic collar at the head junction
attribute_head_type: adjustable pipe wrench head, made of steel, with a curved jaw and visible gear mechanism for adjusting jaw width
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: minor surface wear and slight discoloration on the metal head, no major damage visible
